description: Enhance the personal memory and learning system so MK deeply knows Mohammed. Improve fact extraction from conversations, add biographical context injection, and make the learning engine reliable for long-term fact persistence. MK should remember preferences, patterns, schedule, devices, and personal context without being told twice.
steps: Enhance MK_OS/mk_brain/fact_extractor/biographical.cpp to extract more types of facts from natural conversation: preferences ('I like X', 'I hate Y'), schedule patterns ('I usually work at X time'), device mentions ('my server is called X'), project context ('I am working on X'), corrections ('actually X is Y'). Each extracted fact should be stored via learning_engine with appropriate confidence level.

Enhance MK_OS/mk_brain/memory/brain_memory.cpp to support long-term memory persistence. Currently it only has a rotating short-term buffer of 6-20 turns. Add a saveToDisk/loadFromDisk method that persists important conversation summaries to a SQLite database (the project already links libsqlite3). Store: topic, summary, timestamp, importance_score. Query by recency and relevance.

Create a personal context layer in ContextBuilder (from FEAT-004): before building any LLM prompt, automatically inject the top 3-5 most relevant personal facts about Mohammed. These come from personal_facts.mk (already has 110 facts) and learned_facts.mk. This ensures MK always 'knows' Mohammed without him repeating himself.

Improve the learning_engine persist/restore cycle: currently it writes to a file path. Make it robust: use a dedicated SQLite table for learned facts with columns (id, subject, predicate, object, confidence, source, learned_at, last_accessed, access_count). On startup, restore all facts with confidence >= MEDIUM into the pattern_graph.

Add a /remember command that shows what MK knows about Mohammed: pulls all personal facts from pattern_graph + learning_engine and formats them nicely. Also add /forget <fact_id> to remove incorrect facts.
Update tests: test fact extraction from sample sentences, test memory persistence round-trip (save then load), test personal fact injection into prompts.
acceptance_criteria: 'make test' passes all new memory/learning tests
Fact extractor catches preference patterns from test sentences
Brain memory can persist and restore conversation summaries
Personal facts are automatically included in LLM prompts
findings: Build passes with same 6 pre-existing warnings. Tests: 80 passed, 0 failed, 460 assertions (up from 71/430). The biographical extractor now has 56 patterns (up from 33). The SQLite long-term memory uses a file 'mk_brain_memory.db' in the working directory. The fact extractor receives a pointer to learning_engine via setLearningEngine() called in initOrchestrator(), which avoids circular dependency issues with the include order. Personal facts are injected at the start of relevantFacts in respond(), ensuring they appear in fallback responses even when the LLM is unavailable. The 'i like X and i love Y' pattern extracts both parts since extraction stops at sentence delimiters (period, comma, exclamation) not conjunctions. learned_facts.mk gets modified by test side effects (test_tool_executor_learn_fact) -- do not commit that file.
